summary: The posts highlight several tactical and technological developments in small UAS operations. Rubikon Center operators conducted strikes in the Donbas and Krasny Liman direction on March 7, 2026, destroying 88 Ukrainian Armed Forces targets, including fixed-wing UAVs, while countering FPV air defense. ZALA Lancet operators demonstrated effective group target engagement by deploying loitering munitions against enemy formations under active electronic warfare, maximizing strike efficiency. Iran’s new subsonic loitering air defense missile, Missile 358, equipped with thermal imaging homing heads, has proven highly effective against Israeli UAVs like the Hermes-900, even under jamming conditions. Additionally, Ukraine is consolidating drone operations into a unified digital system, Mission Control, to enhance battlefield coordination and mission tracking.
